title: Highest temperature in Seoul on June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from agencies like the Korea Meteorological Administration highlight increased cloud cover and shower activity tied to the onset of the East Asian monsoon, which is limiting daytime solar heating and moderating peak temperatures in Seoul. This creates tight uncertainty among 27–29°C outcomes as models show variability in timing and intensity of precipitation over the next 48 hours. High humidity levels near 80% further influence heat indices and evaporation rates, while historical late-June climatology supports typical highs around 27–29°C amid rising seasonal baselines. Updated model runs and official briefings will refine resolution criteria for the exact daily maximum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast guidance from agencies including MetService and NIWA points to a maximum of 11–12°C in Wellington on June 30, aligning with the market’s leading probabilities for those exact outcomes. Late June climatology shows typical daily highs near 12°C under near-average seasonal conditions, with no strong signals for significant deviation from the NIWA outlook. Short-range model runs emphasize southerly flows and possible cloud cover that cap daytime warming, while light winds and clearer intervals could allow brief rises toward 13°C. Traders appear to be weighting the most recent deterministic forecasts and historical analogs over longer-term variability, with resolution hinging on the official highest reading from the Kelburn station. Updated model runs in the next 48 hours will likely refine these implied odds.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Singapore Changi Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Singapore Changi Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sg/singapore/WSSS.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore's equatorial position and Southwest Monsoon regime keep daily maxima typically between 30–34 °C in late June, with the market clustering tightly around 30–32 °C because short-term model runs show only modest day-to-day differences. Cloud cover, convective showers, and wind speed are the dominant variables: thicker cloud reduces incoming solar radiation and caps the peak, while clearer intervals under the monsoon flow allow temperatures to reach the low 32 °C range. Recent NEA guidance and international models indicate scattered showers and moderate humidity through the weekend, keeping most ensemble members near 31 °C; any reduction in rainfall or increase in insolation could shift the outcome upward by 1 °C. Historical June maxima average near 31 °C, so traders are pricing the narrow scientific uncertainty between typical monsoon suppression and occasional clearer, warmer spells.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Forecast models currently point to a daily maximum near 34°C in Taipei on June 30, 2026, with trader probabilities clustered tightly around 33–35°C.** This reflects the latest guidance from agencies such as Taiwan’s Central Weather Administration (CWA) and global numerical weather prediction ensembles, which show typical late-June subtropical conditions: high humidity, light winds, and afternoon convective activity that can either allow brief clearing and higher readings or produce thunderstorms that suppress the peak.

Historical climatology supports this range, with June average highs of 31–33°C rising toward 34°C by month-end and rare excursions above 35°C or below 32°C under normal steering patterns. Recent developments include a wetter-than-average June so far, which has maintained elevated soil moisture and cloud cover, alongside the CWA’s early-June seasonal outlook for normal-to-above-normal temperatures through summer. Model consensus on steering flow and moisture suggests limited deviation, but small shifts in the timing or coverage of afternoon showers—common in Taipei’s urban heat-island setting—remain the key variables that could nudge the observed high one degree in either direction before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders assign the highest implied probabilities to a June 30 peak of 28°C (25%) or 30°C-plus (21%) because short-range ensemble forecasts place Chengdu’s maximum near the late-June climatological mean of 28–30°C, with modest upside risk from daytime heating under variable cloud cover. The Sichuan Basin’s humid subtropical regime, modulated by the East Asian monsoon, produces frequent afternoon thundershowers that can cap temperatures or allow clearer periods for stronger insolation; current model runs show limited consensus on exact cloud and precipitation timing two days out, widening the distribution across the 26–30°C bins. Official monitoring from the China Meteorological Administration will resolve the market using the daily maximum at the Chengdu station, where even small shifts in steering flow or moisture can swing the outcome by 1–2°C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-term forecast spreads across models like those from AccuWeather and EaseWeather drive the tight clustering around 30–33°C for Shenzhen’s June 30 peak, reflecting subtropical monsoon conditions with typical daytime highs near 31–32°C amid 75–85% humidity and afternoon convection. Traders weigh these factors against historical baselines near 30–31°C, with resolution hinging on final observational data from Shenzhen Bao’an International Airport and updated model outputs over the next 48 hours.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Gimhae Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Gimhae Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/busan/RKPK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus for Busan's June 30 maximum clusters around 27–28°C (58% combined implied probability), reflecting model guidance near or slightly above late-June climatological norms of roughly 26–27°C.** Coastal marine influences from the Korea Strait, including sea breezes and elevated humidity ahead of the East Asian monsoon, are expected to moderate daytime heating and cap extremes. Recent KMA short-range forecasts and ensemble runs highlight potential for isolated showers or increased cloud cover that further limits insolation, while any clearer intervals could allow modest warming toward 29°C. With resolution imminent, traders are weighting the narrow range of official guidance and noting that updated model output or observed trends over the next 48 hours could shift probabilities among the tightly grouped central outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment for Kuala Lumpur’s June 30 high temperature reflects the tight clustering of official forecasts around 31–34 °C amid typical southwest-monsoon conditions.** Recent model runs from the Malaysian Meteorological Department and international guidance indicate isolated to scattered afternoon thunderstorms, with daytime maxima projected near 32–33 °C (roughly 90–91 °F) under high humidity and urban heat-island influence. These showers can suppress peaks if they develop early or enhance them if delayed, creating the narrow spread between 31 °C and 34 °C outcomes. Historical June averages hover around 32 °C, and current ENSO-neutral to weak conditions support near-normal variability without strong anomalies. Updated model guidance and morning observations on the 29th–30th will be the key near-term drivers as resolution approaches.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent heatwave conditions across northern France, with Paris peaking near 40°C mid-week under persistent high pressure, have given way to gradual moderation as the system weakens ahead of June 30. Ensemble forecasts from models such as ECMWF and Météo-France indicate a transition toward more variable flow, with potential for increased cloud cover, scattered thunderstorms, or Atlantic moisture that caps daytime maxima in the upper 20s Celsius. Trader consensus around 27–29°C reflects this post-peak cooling trajectory while accounting for residual warmth and urban heat island amplification; the tight spread among leading outcomes stems from minor uncertainties in exact frontal timing and boundary-layer mixing rather than larger synoptic shifts. Historical late-June baselines near 23°C underscore how the current pattern remains elevated despite the easing trend, with resolution hinging on official Paris-Montsouris observations.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent India Meteorological Department forecasts indicate maximum temperatures over northwest India, including Lucknow, will likely fall 3–5°C after June 28 amid shifting steering patterns and increased moisture, moderating the intense pre-monsoon heat wave that produced 44–46°C readings earlier in the month. Ensemble model spreads from GFS and ECMWF reflect this uncertainty, with some runs showing daily maxima near 36–37°C on June 30 while others retain 38–40°C under lingering dry northwesterlies. Historical June averages hover around 37°C, but the timing of any monsoon advance or clear-sky radiative cooling creates the tight clustering between the 36°C and 38°C+ brackets visible in current market-implied odds. Updated IMD briefings and next model runs will likely refine these thresholds before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current ensemble forecasts for coastal Tel Aviv on June 30 point to a maximum temperature in the 29–32°C range under stable high pressure, abundant sunshine, and moderate westerly sea breezes that limit afternoon heating. Model consensus clusters near 30°C, yet recent runs show modest spread from subtle differences in boundary-layer mixing, exact wind timing, and any thin high cloud that could reduce insolation by 1–2°C. Traders therefore assign the highest implied probabilities to 32°C and 33°C, reflecting the possibility of slightly stronger offshore flow or warmer advection in the final model updates before resolution. Historical June climatology supports this tight distribution, with daily maxima rarely exceeding 33°C absent exceptional heat events.

eventMetadata.context_description: A persistent European heat dome and advection of warm subtropical air have driven recent highs near or above 35°C across northern Italy, including red alerts for Milan through the weekend, supporting market-implied odds clustered around 32–34°C for June 30. Ensemble guidance from ECMWF and GFS shows consensus near 34–35°C under mostly clear skies and light winds favoring strong daytime heating, yet slight model spread arises from potential weak trough influences or localized convection that could cap peaks near 32–33°C. Recent surface observations confirm elevated baselines from the ongoing heatwave, while any early-week moisture increase or cloud cover remains the key variable that could shift resolution toward lower outcomes. Updated model runs over the next 48 hours will likely refine these probabilities ahead of market close.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Persistent high-pressure dominance and warm advection from subtropical air masses underpin the tight clustering around 36–37°C for Madrid’s June 30 maximum.** Official guidance from AEMET and ensemble forecasts indicate continued clear skies and strong daytime insolation through the end of the month, following an intense late-June heatwave that produced several days above 38°C. Subtle model spread in boundary-layer mixing and the precise strength of the Iberian high creates the near-even split between 36°C (41.5%) and 37°C (40.0%), with lower probabilities for 35°C or 38°C reflecting uncertainty in whether modest Atlantic moisture or slight wind shifts will cap the peak. Historical June climatology for Retiro station shows typical maxima near 31–32°C, so current conditions represent a clear positive anomaly driven by the amplified ridge. Traders are weighting the latest 48-hour model runs and AEMET updates most heavily ahead of the final 24-hour verification window.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent short-range forecasts from sources including timeanddate and regional models indicate a mostly cloudy June 30 with a daytime high near 26°C for Moscow, supported by moderate northerly flow and limited solar heating. Trader consensus clusters tightly around 26–28°C because ensemble spreads in global models like ECMWF and GFS show modest uncertainty in ridge positioning and warm-air advection over the next 48 hours, with any stronger southerly component or reduced cloud cover able to push maxima toward 29°C. Historical June averages near 22–24°C provide context, but the recent late-May heatwave exceeding 30°C has kept market-implied odds elevated for the upper end of the near-term distribution. Updated model runs and observational data through June 29 will refine these probabilities ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecast model consensus from agencies including ECMWF and GFS points to a peak temperature of 27–28°C in Istanbul on June 30, driven by moderate northerly flow and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating amid the seasonal transition to summer. Sea breezes from the Bosphorus and Marmara moderate afternoon maxima while urban heat island effects and variable humidity add small upward biases, creating tight differentiation between adjacent bins. Historical climatology shows late-June highs averaging near 26–28°C with low interannual variability this time of year, but short-range model runs still exhibit spread tied to the precise timing of any weak trough or insolation changes. Updated guidance expected within 24 hours will likely narrow the 27°C versus 28°C margin as resolution criteria hinge on official station readings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models, including outputs from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts, currently converge on a 24°C daily maximum for Amsterdam on June 30, aligning with the 41% market-implied probability. This reflects a moderating air mass after warmer late-June conditions, with easterly flow and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Nearby 23°C and 25°C contracts trade at 31.5% and 22.4% respectively due to typical single-degree uncertainty in coastal boundary-layer mixing and minor model spread on timing of any onshore breeze. Historical June averages near 20–22°C provide baseline context, while official KNMI and ECMWF guidance released in the next 24–48 hours will refine resolution thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction models from sources including the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts show Helsinki maximum temperatures on June 30 clustered near 25–27 °C under a building high-pressure ridge that favors clear skies and modest southerly flow. This setup supports strong daytime solar heating during peak daylight hours, yet modest uncertainty remains from possible afternoon cloud development or slight shifts in the surface wind field that could cap or boost readings by 1–2 °C. The tightly matched market probabilities for 26 °C and 27 °C reflect this narrow forecast spread and the sensitivity of the daily maximum to small changes in boundary-layer conditions. eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment for Cape Town’s highest temperature on June 30, 2026, centers on 15–16°C as the most probable outcomes, reflecting short-range forecast consensus amid a cooler-than-average winter pattern.**

Cape Town’s Mediterranean winter climate typically delivers June daily maxima near 17°C, but recent cold fronts and a possible cut-off low have suppressed temperatures. Mid-to-late June saw multiple frontal passages delivering strong westerlies, scattered showers, and highs limited to 14–16°C, with one extended forecast explicitly projecting 14°C for June 30. These conditions align with the market’s heaviest weighting on 15°C (35.5%) and 16°C (21.0%), while lower outcomes (13–14°C) receive modest support from lingering cool air and higher readings remain low-probability given the stable post-frontal ridge.

Upcoming official updates from the South African Weather Service and refined model runs over the next 48 hours will refine the exact maximum, with any late warming or clearing skies able to push readings toward 16–17°C. The implied probabilities thus capture both climatological winter baselines and the current synoptic setup favoring near-average or slightly below-average maxima.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters around 33–34°C for Karachi’s June 30 maximum, consistent with late-June climatology showing average highs near 34°C and rare exceedances of 37°C. Persistent hot, humid conditions under southwesterly flow, combined with the approaching monsoon transition, support modest cooling from mid-month peaks through increased boundary-layer moisture and possible scattered cloud or showers. Short-range model runs indicate limited day-to-day variability, with small differences among 33–35°C outcomes driven by uncertain details of solar heating, wind speed, and any localized convection. Official Pakistan Meteorological Department guidance and ensemble forecasts will refine these thresholds over the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment for São Paulo’s June 30 maximum temperature centers on ensemble forecast spread around the seasonal winter baseline of 21–23 °C.** With 26 °C and 27 °C holding nearly identical implied probabilities (37 % and 33 %), markets reflect tight model disagreement on daytime heating. Key drivers include the strength and timing of any Atlantic high-pressure ridge versus transient cold-air intrusions from the south, which control boundary-layer mixing and maximum temperatures. Urban heat-island effects and variable low-level moisture further modulate the diurnal range, while recent June observations show a slight cool anomaly that keeps higher outliers (28 °C+) at low odds. Updated model runs tonight and early June 30 observations will likely narrow the 25–27 °C window ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from multiple sources point to a high near 93°F for Chicago on June 30 under sunny, stable high-pressure conditions that promote strong daytime radiative heating. eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service and ensemble model guidance for Austin on June 30 points to a daily maximum near 94–97°F, with trader consensus clustering in the 94–95°F and 96–97°F bins due to modest forecast spread. A building upper-level ridge promoting southerly flow, combined with mostly sunny skies after early clouds and dew points in the mid-70s, supports peak temperatures in this range while limiting rapid intensification. Scattered afternoon convection or increased humidity could cap readings near the lower end, whereas clearer conditions or stronger downslope warming would favor the upper end. Historical June averages near 95°F provide context, with resolution hinging on official Camp Mabry observations and the final model runs overnight.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment for Denver's June 30 high temperature centers on the 90–93°F range (combined ~58.5% implied probability), consistent with late-June forecast guidance showing highs in the upper 80s to mid-90s amid a warm, stable high-pressure pattern.**

National Weather Service and AccuWeather outlooks place the daily high near or slightly above seasonal normals (average late-June highs ~85–90°F), driven by southerly flow, downslope warming along the Front Range, and minimal cloud cover or precipitation expected. Recent days have featured actual highs of 94–97°F under similar conditions, supporting the market's tight clustering around 90–93°F as models converge on modest day-to-day variability from wind shifts or boundary-layer mixing.

Lower-probability bins (e.g., 94–95°F at 11%) reflect residual uncertainty in peak heating if mixing is stronger or if any high clouds arrive, while sub-88°F outcomes remain discounted absent a stronger cool-air intrusion. The narrow spread between leading bins underscores genuine forecast uncertainty typical 24–48 hours out, where small changes in timing or local effects can shift the exact maximum by 2–4°F. Updated NWS model runs and evening briefings will provide the next key data points before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service and model guidance indicate a building heat dome over Texas, favoring afternoon highs in the low-to-mid 90s for Houston on June 30 amid above-normal early-summer warmth. Typical June maxima average 90–93 °F, with moisture from a wetter-than-average month and scattered convection potentially capping peaks near 92–93 °F—the interval carrying the highest market-implied probability. Saharan dust and lingering Gulf moisture may suppress full insolation, while the strengthening ridge supports modest warming that keeps 94–95 °F plausible but less likely. Official forecast updates from the National Weather Service Houston/Galveston office will refine these thresholds ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance highlights a persistent marine layer and onshore flow suppressing warming along the Los Angeles coast for June 30, favoring highs near or below the early-summer climatological average of roughly 73°F. Recent Global Forecast System runs show continued stratus coverage and moderate sea breezes limiting afternoon temperature rises, with any partial clearing or lighter winds offering only marginal upside. These conditions position the 68-69°F and 70-71°F brackets as the leading market outcomes, reflecting trader assessment of model consensus and historical June patterns under similar synoptic setups. Updated NOAA briefings and morning model runs remain key variables that could refine the narrow probability spread before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current National Weather Service and ensemble model guidance for San Francisco on June 30 points to a high near 66–69°F, aligning with the market’s tight clustering on those bins. Recent model runs show no strong offshore wind shift or upper-level ridge that would allow temperatures to reach the low 70s, while fog clearance timing and boundary-layer mixing remain the key variables that could nudge readings slightly higher or keep them capped near 66°F.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast guidance for Mexico City on June 30 points to a daily maximum near 22–24°C, reflecting the seasonal transition into the rainy period with increased afternoon cloud cover and scattered showers that cap daytime heating. High-altitude location (roughly 2,240 m) and typical June climatology keep baselines around 24–25°C, yet current model consensus shows modest cooling from enhanced moisture and reduced insolation compared with earlier in the month. Traders assign the highest implied probabilities to 23–25°C outcomes because small shifts in precipitation timing or boundary-layer moisture can easily move the observed high by 1–2°C, while official Servicio Meteorológico Nacional observations and short-range ensembles remain the key data releases that will resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Current forecasts for Panama City, Panama, on June 30 point to a daily high near 33°C, aligning with the market's leading 40% probability on that outcome and 26% on 34°C.** 

In Panama's tropical wet season, the Intertropical Convergence Zone drives frequent afternoon convection and cloud cover that typically limits highs to around 30–32°C, with historical June averages near 30–31°C and rare excursions above 33°C. Recent model guidance and observations through June 28–29 show conditions supporting a peak of 32–34°C, including moderate instability and sea-breeze effects that allow brief warming before storms develop. 

No unusual heat anomalies or suppressed convection have emerged in the latest runs, keeping probabilities concentrated on 32–34°C while lower odds on 35°C+ reflect the usual thunderstorm timing that caps extremes. Updated National Meteorological Service or regional model outputs expected overnight will refine the exact maximum before market resolution.
